title: Notification on behalf of the app rather than from user when using token
excerpt: sending notifications to user from api using oauth token shows that the notification is from the user who authorized the token and not from app, there should be an option to allow app developers to send it like its from …

posts.author: Nir-Jetpack (Nir @ Jetpack Team)
posts.cookedHtml: sending notifications to user from api using oauth token shows that the notification is from the user who authorized the token and not from app, there should be an option to allow app developers to send it like its from the app (like it shows when we send notifications using short lived token)

posts.author: benhowes (Ben Howes 🐨)
posts.cookedHtml:
This! I’ve even had to suggest to users that they create a “service user” in order to get around notifications being from the user who set up the automation/app. In this usecase, the app was able to let another user know when an item moved to a new status.

It would also be helpful to consider this in the newer Emails & Activities APIs - at present, logging an activity always appears to be from the user who authed the app. I will have to create a way to get all users to log in to the app and then pick the correct auth token in order to log activity as the correct user.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: The same should apply to updates, so the update comes from the app. Which unfortunately even with short live tokens comes from the user. Or at least an option

posts.author: allanl (Allan)
posts.cookedHtml: Upvoted! This will be useful for us too.
